concerns.text: Task T019 ('Implement power_spectrum.py to compute spherical-harmonic coefficients a_lm from residuals (Observed - Expected)') contradicts the plan.md 'Statistical Methodology Note' which mandates using the exposure-corrected intensity map (N_obs/N_exp) with shot-noise subtraction. T019 describes a consumer task (power spectrum) that relies on a producer task (exposure correction) defined in T020, but the description in T019 implies a different (and statistically invalid) data flow (simple subtraction) that does not match the artifact produced by T020. This creates a semantic dependency violation where the consumer expects an artifact format different from what the producer emits.

Task T020 ('Implement exposure-corrected intensity map... in analysis/healpix_conversion.py') is placed AFTER T019 (power spectrum) in the list, yet T019 explicitly requires the 'residuals' or 'intensity' data to function. The plan.md states the analysis uses the intensity map to compute the spectrum. T020 must precede T019 to satisfy the producer-before-consumer dependency. Currently, T019 attempts to consume an artifact (exposure-corrected map) that T020 has not yet produced.

Task T023 ('Integrate with User Story 1 components to consume generated HEALPix maps') is listed after T019-T022. However, T019 and T020 (Power Spectrum and Exposure Correction) are the actual consumers of the US1 HEALPix maps. T023 is a generic integration task that should logically precede the specific implementation of the consumers (T019/T020) or be merged into them. As written, it implies a dependency on T019/T020 completion, but the data flow requires the US1 maps to be available BEFORE T019/T020 can run. This creates a circular or inverted dependency in the task list.

Task T032 ('Integrate with User Story 2 to consume observed C_l values') is listed after T027-T031 (Monte Carlo and Significance Test). However, T028 (Significance Test) explicitly requires the observed C_l values as input. T032 is a generic integration task that should precede T028. The current ordering suggests T028 runs before the integration of US2 data is complete, violating the producer (US2 C_l) before consumer (US3 Significance Test) rule.

Task T005 is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but involves 'checksum verification logic'. If this logic depends on the existence of files created by T004 (config.yaml) or T001 (project structure), it may not be truly parallel-safe without explicit dependency declarations. While likely safe if T001/T004 are 'setup' tasks, the [P] tag should be verified against the actual file I/O dependencies to ensure no race conditions on the `data/` directory initialization.
